evidence: Each frame carries one focus: the lilac meter dial with its needle and score readout, the sentence entering last as the resolution; nothing competes at equal weight.
At most the dial, needle, three region labels, and the a·b score appear together, and they build progressively across frames rather than flooding in at once.
The labels oppose/ignore/agree sit directly on the dial arc, and a·b sits on the score readout; there is no boxed legend or corner key.
The only sentence, 'the dot product is a similarity meter', annotates the visible dial directly below it; other text is short labels on objects.
All text — region labels, the a·b annotation, the +2.40 readout, and the tagline — is rendered well above the ~2.2% frame-height threshold.
The dial, needle, score, and sentence all sit fully inside the frame with comfortable margins.
Labels, arrows, dial, and text are cleanly separated with no overlapping or stamped-over elements.
Periwinkle and amber mark the two probe vectors consistently, lilac carries the meter dial/needle and the a·b readout throughout, and the resolved sentence gets a mint fit-family glow.
Every element — vectors, dial, needle, labels, score, sentence — carries propositional content; no decorative filler is present.
The stills collapse into a lilac meter dial whose needle swings oppose→ignore→agree, the a·b annotation sits on the score readout, and the aha sentence trues up with a mint glow, matching the intent.
The a·b label and score sit directly under the needle, and the sentence sits under the dial it describes.
Across frames the probe stills give way to the arc, the needle, the score sweep, and finally the sentence, reading as a continuous build in reasoning order.
The composition is centered with generous negative space and a clear top-to-bottom scan path from dial to score to sentence.
